message:
feat(session): implement thread-safe SessionHandle for extension access

Major refactor to enable extensions to safely access session state:

SessionHandle (src/session.rs):
- New wrapper type: SessionHandle(pub Arc<Mutex<Session>>)
- Implements ExtensionSession trait with async methods:
  - get_state(): Returns sessionFile and sessionName as JSON
  - get_messages(): Returns all SessionMessage entries from history
  - get_entries(): Returns all session entries as JSON values
  - get_branch(): Returns entries for current branch path
  - set_name(): Updates session name in memory
  - append_message(): Adds new message to session
  - append_custom_entry(): Adds custom typed entries
- Each method acquires the mutex via Cx::for_request() context

AgentSession (src/agent.rs):
- Changed session field from Session to Arc<Mutex<Session>>
- Updated constructor signature to accept Arc<Mutex<Session>>
- init_extensions() now binds SessionHandle to ExtensionManager
- save_and_index() and save() acquire lock before saving
- Dispatch startup event with session path from locked session

This architecture allows JS extensions to call pi.session.* methods
which dispatch to SessionHandle, enabling read/write access to session
state without blocking the agent loop. The mutex ensures thread-safe
access when multiple async operations touch the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
